name: Service tokens (M2M) — approval frames
description: Contract-freeze UX artifacts for the vendor-neutral machine-to-machine service-token surface (create-scoped → reveal-once secret → list → revoke) backed by FuzeFront's Security API. Approving a flow approves its visual + interaction model AND the component/package build inventory before UI implementation. frontend-test-engineer drives Playwright against these via the data-frame / data-* hooks. The reveal-once screen (03) is the most important in the flow.

commissionedByApproval: last_used_at on the token record (frames a, d) — not backed today; column reads '—' until it exists
bring the token CRUD endpoints into the frozen packages/security/openapi.yaml (only POST /v1/security/tokens issue + introspect live there now)

frames.label: (a) Service tokens
frames.summary: Token list: name + prefix, scope chips, created, last-used, per-row revoke.
frames.acceptanceNotes: Rows from GET /api/organizations/{orgId}/tokens exposing name, token_prefix, scopes, created_at, expires_at — never the raw secret. 'Last used' is not backed yet (no last_used_at field); it renders '—' until commissioned, never fabricated activity. No vendor name in the DOM.

frames.label: (b) Create token
frames.summary: Name + scope checkboxes + optional expiry. Submit disabled until a name and at least one scope are present.
frames.acceptanceNotes: POST /api/organizations/{orgId}/tokens with { name, scopes[], expires_at? } → 201 with the raw secret returned once (frame c). Scope options should render from the org scope catalogue; the four shown are illustrative Resource:action strings. Fail-closed: no zero-scope token can be created.

frames.label: (c) Secret shown once — MOST IMPORTANT
frames.summary: The one-time reveal: full secret with copy affordance, an unrecoverable warning, and an explicit ack gating Done.
frames.acceptanceNotes: The raw secret is the 'token' field returned ONLY on the 201 create; the backend stores a hash and never returns it again. Copy writes to clipboard (data-action='copy-secret' → data-copy-status). Done stays disabled (data-requires-ack) until data-ack-checkbox is ticked. The displayed secret MUST be an obviously-fake placeholder (ff_tok_EXAMPLE_NOT_A_REAL_TOKEN); a real impl never logs or re-renders it and discards it on navigation.

frames.label: (d) Revoke token
frames.summary: Ordinary confirm (d1), plus type-to-confirm escalation when the token is in active use (d2).
frames.acceptanceNotes: DELETE /api/organizations/{orgId}/tokens/{tokenId} → 204 (idempotent), immediate. d2 gates Revoke behind a name match (data-requires-name-match) for a token showing recent use. 'Active use' derives from the same last-used signal frame (a) commissions; until it exists, treat every token as ordinary (d1).

frames.label: (e) States
frames.summary: Loading skeleton, no-tokens invitation, load error+retry, and 403 forbidden shown in place.
frames.acceptanceNotes: Loading = aria-busy skeleton for in-flight GET tokens. Empty is the first-run invitation to create. Error shows data-action='retry'. A 403 is an AUTHORIZATION denial rendered in place — NEVER a redirect to sign-in (only a 401 re-authenticates).
